queries_sent.query: how do government agencies implement study methodology, required data information programs
how do government agencies implement Inventory quality products services across programs
how do government agencies implement Study classifications standards relevant sector/activity
how do government agencies implement stakeholders current landscape products services programs
how do government agencies implement Comprehensive study methodology document data programs
how do government agencies implement List local/international market experts sector/activity programs
needs assessment methodology for government service agencies
service portfolio design framework for public agencies

coverage_assessment:
The evidence pack provides solid coverage across four of the five core RFP scope areas: (1) Study Methodology — well covered by EXT-001 (government agency mixed-methods frameworks) and EXT-006 (CNA methodology); (2) Inventory of Quality Products and Services — well covered by EXT-002 (government inventory management best practices); (3) Classifications and Standards — covered by EXT-003 (NAICS/ISIC/NACE international standards) and EXT-008 (sector-specific HTA institutionalization); (4) Stakeholder and Landscape Analysis — covered by EXT-004 (multi-stakeholder engagement frameworks) and EXT-010 (collaborative innovation in service design); (5) Developmental Needs and Innovative Services — partially covered by EXT-006, EXT-007 (zakat/non-profit service models), and EXT-005 (service innovation portfolio management).

KEY GAPS IDENTIFIED:
- Saudi Arabia-specific non-profit sector data: No local Saudi evidence was found on the current landscape of fundraising, volunteering, endowment (waqf), or hosting services. This is a significant evidence gap that will require primary research or consultation with local experts (e.g., MCSA, Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority, Saudi Vision 2030 reports).
- International country-specific non-profit sector benchmarks: The RFP requires analysis of leading practices in no fewer than 5 countries. The current evidence covers Australia (EXT-005), USA (EXT-001, EXT-002, EXT-003), and Indonesia (EXT-007, EXT-010) but lacks dedicated sources for GCC/MENA comparators, UK, Canada, or other relevant jurisdictions.
- Waqf (endowment) and volunteering sector-specific quality frameworks: No dedicated academic or industry sources were found on quality standards for waqf management or volunteering program design at an international level.
- Market expert identification methodology: No specific evidence was found on how to identify and engage market experts for non-profit sector studies, representing a gap in the work plan section.
- Non-profit sector economic activity classification: No source specifically addresses how non-profit activities are classified within national economic classification systems (e.g., ICNPO — International Classification of Non-Profit Organizations), which is a critical gap for the sector inventory task.

Evidence Tier Breakdown: primary_source_count: 6, secondary_source_count: 4, analogical_source_count: 0
